items.headline: Lam Research (LRCX) Is Betting On Advanced Packaging For 50% Growth In 2026
items.summary: Lam Research is highlighting its advanced packaging business as a key growth driver, with the company projecting revenues from this segment to rise more than 50% in 2026. The push is linked to demand for AI chips, high bandwidth memory and chiplet architectures, areas that require more complex packaging solutions. Competition is intensifying, with rivals such as Applied Materials and KLA Corporation also targeting advanced packaging opportunities. Lam Research, traded as NasdaqGS:LRCX, is...

items.headline: Why Applied Materials Inc (AMAT) Is Making Big Moves in Singapore
items.summary: Applied Materials Inc (NASDAQ:AMAT) is one of billionaire Ken Fisher’s top high growth stock picks. items.headline: EssilorLuxottica, Applied Materials strike deal to develop smart glasses, AR technology
items.summary: EssilorLuxottica, the world's largest eyewear maker, has signed a long-term ‌deal with chipmaking equipment company Applied ‌Materials to develop augmented reality display technology and AI ​glasses, the companies said on Tuesday. items.headline: How Much Upside Can LRCX Stock's Growth Deliver?
items.summary: At $388.92, Lam Research (LRCX) looks set up for roughly 51% of upside over the next three years under a conservative scenario. That is a move large enough to justify digging into where it comes from. Revenue compounding does the work, but the multiple takes a meaningful cut along the way.
